title: Head of Revenue Operations
description: Reporting to the CRO, this hands-on leadership role builds and scales the operational backbone of Finout's go-to-market engine as the company enters its next growth phase. Requires 7+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Ops, or Strategy within high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce administration and revenue analytics expertise, and strong cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR. Experience with globally distributed teams collaborating across time zones, including the Tel Aviv HQ, is essential. A strategic, analytical leader-doer with a builder's mentality.
score_reason: Omri is a perfect match with 15+ years B2B SaaS RevOps expertise, deep Salesforce skills, and proven experience scaling high-growth companies well beyond $30M ARR. The Tel Aviv hybrid setup aligns perfectly with his location preferences, and his cross-functional leadership background directly matches Finout's need for a strategic, hands-on builder reporting to the CRO.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years building and scaling RevOps functions in high-growth B2B SaaS companies, including managing Salesforce-driven operations at scale beyond $30M ARR. Your Head of Revenue Operations role at Finout caught my attention because it combines the hands-on builder mentality I thrive on with a strategic platform play in the FinOps space—plus the Tel Aviv hybrid setup is exactly where I'm based and operating. I'd love to explore how my experience scaling GTM operations across distributed teams could help accelerate Finout's next growth phase.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Build GTM systems in an AI-first way at an established company with a strong inbound-driven business and significant outbound systems to build. Requires 5+ years in data, software engineering, revenue operations, or similar technical roles in B2B SaaS. Skills include complex workflow design (software and no-code), API integrations, prompt engineering, and RAG/vector database experience using Supabase, Pinecone, or similar. You'll build autonomous AI systems that research prospects, craft personalized messages, manage follow-up sequences at scale, and develop lead scoring and enrichment pipelines using waterfall enrichment methodology.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer role combining RevOps expertise with AI-native systems building. Omri's 15+ years in B2B SaaS GTM and pipeline forecasting background map well to autonomous AI systems, workflow design, and lead scoring—though hands-on prompt engineering and vector database experience may need validation.
ai_opener: Tempo's vision of building GTM systems in an AI-first way resonates deeply with my 15+ years scaling revenue operations in B2B SaaS. I've architected complex pipeline forecasting and enrichment workflows that could translate directly to your autonomous prospect research and personalized outreach systems—particularly interested in how you're applying RAG and vector databases to waterfall enrichment at scale.

title: GTM Engineer, Revenue Operations
description: Design and maintain a robust GTM tech stack to streamline outbound prospecting and minimize manual efforts. Identify repetitive tasks and implement automated workflows, develop reporting mechanisms and ROI analyses, and ensure data accuracy across GTM systems. Works closely with Sales, Revenue Operations, and Technology teams; reports to the Director of Revenue Systems & Processes. Requires extensive experience in a revenue operations or systems role, ideally in B2B SaaS. Offers asynchronous work flexibility. Base salary range $40,900 to $92,050 USD depending on location and experience.
score_reason: Exceptional alignment with Omri's core RevOps expertise, GTM tech stack design, and B2B SaaS background. Remote global role matches location preferences perfectly, though salary range ($40.9-92k USD) may be below market for a 15+ year senior executive.
ai_opener: I noticed you're building out a GTM tech stack focused on automation and data integrity—exactly the kind of systems-driven work I've spent 15+ years perfecting across high-growth B2B SaaS companies. With my background architecting Salesforce/HubSpot ecosystems and designing pipeline forecasting workflows, I'd be interested in exploring how I could help you scale your revenue systems while keeping the team lean and efficient.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Join Supabase's Growth and RevOps teams to make go-to-market teams dramatically more productive. Design and build automation across the GTM stack (HubSpot, BigQuery, Hightouch, enrichment tools), own and extend PLS trigger infrastructure, maintain HubSpot architecture, build internal tools and dashboards, and integrate AI (Claude, Gemini) into rep workflows. Requires 3+ years in GTM Engineering, Sales Engineering, or RevOps Engineering at a B2B SaaS company, comfort with HubSpot APIs, SQL/BigQuery, and building automations with Python or Node.js. Fully remote with ESOP and tech allowance.
score_reason: Exceptional match across all dimensions: senior RevOps/GTM expertise directly aligns with GTM Engineer role, HubSpot mastery is core requirement, and AI-native GTM integration (Claude/Gemini into workflows) matches Omri's stated strengths. Remote global position removes any location friction.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years building GTM infrastructure at scale, and Supabase's vision of making go-to-market teams dramatically more productive through AI-native automation really resonates. Your stack (HubSpot, BigQuery, Hightouch + Claude/Gemini integration) is exactly what I've been architecting—I'd love to explore how we can take your PLS triggers and rep workflows to the next level.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Take Jiga's revenue operations from scrappy to systematic, building systems that turn a layered GTM motion (inbound, word-of-mouth, product-led, account expansion, targeted outbound) into a repeatable engine. Requires 4-6 years in rev ops, sales ops, or GTM ops at a B2B company through a scaling phase. Skills include Clay enrichment workflows, CRM workflows (Attio), API connections, basic scripting, and AI workflow building. A high-autonomy IC role serving enterprise customers like NASA, Google, Tesla, and Apple in manufacturing/procurement.

The modern AI/Clay/Attio tech stack matches well, though may require some tool-specific ramp.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Jiga and thought of you immediately—taking a multi-motion GTM (inbound, PLG, outbound, expansion) from scrappy to repeatable is exactly the kind of systematization work you've scaled through at growth-stage B2B companies. Your pipeline forecasting and ops architecture experience could accelerate Jiga's ability to serve customers like NASA and Google with predictable revenue motion. title: GTM Engineer
description: As the founding GTM Engineer reporting to the Head of AI GTM, you'll build AI-native lead qualification, enrichment, routing, and reporting on Clay, powered by Claude and other LLMs. The role starts at roughly 60% AI systems and 40% traditional marketing ops, shifting toward 100% AI over time. Build Clay workflows that find, enrich, score, and route leads using intent signals and firmographic data, merge data sources into one scoring model, and build custom integrations, APIs, and webhooks. Values AI agent experience over traditional marketing ops tenure.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer title, AI-native focus, and RevOps technical depth. However, the role's emphasis on AI agent experience over traditional ops tenure and founding-stage positioning (likely smaller team) creates some misalignment with Omri's senior executive profile and preference for established structures.

The shift from traditional marketing ops to Claude-powered Clay workflows is exactly where I see the category heading. I'd love to explore how my pipeline forecasting and RevOps infrastructure experience could accelerate your GTM systems as you scale from 60% to 100% AI.
